system_prompt: You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to review the provided error logs and diagnose the underlying issues. You will be provided with a similar case reference, which is a list of similar cases that are ordered by similarity. You can use this reference to help you understand the user requirement and the error.When an error indicates that a specific keyword is undefined (for example, 'div(phi,(p|rho)) is undefined'), your response must propose a solution that simply defines that exact keyword as shown in the error log. Do not reinterpret or modify the keyword (e.g., do not treat '|' as 'or'); instead, assume it is meant to be taken literally. Propose ideas on how to resolve the errors, but do not modify any files directly. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. Do not ask the user any questions.The user will supply all relevant foam files along with the error logs, and within the logs, you will find both the error content and the corresponding error command indicated by the log file name.
user_prompt:
<similar_case_reference><index>
case name: counterFlowFlame2D
case domain: combustion
case category: laminar
case solver: reactingFoam
</index>
<directory_structure>
<dir>directory name: 0. File names in this directory: [CH4, CO2, H2O, N2, O2, T, U, Ydefault, alphat, p]</dir>
<dir>directory name: system. File names in this directory: [blockMeshDict, controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution]</dir>
<dir>directory name: constant. File names in this directory: [chemistryProperties, combustionProperties, momentumTransport, physicalProperties, reactions, thermo.compressibleGas]</dir>
</directory_structure></similar_case_reference>
<similar_case_advice>match_level='high' use_scope='All files from the reference case counterFlowFlame2D can be used as templates, including blockMeshDict (adjust domain to 0.02m × 0.02m), boundary conditions in 0/ directory (set fuel inlet velocity to 0.1 m/s and air inlet velocity to -0.2 m/s), thermophysicalProperties and chemistryProperties (ensure CH4/O2/N2 species and appropriate reaction mechanism), and controlDict (set endTime=0.5, deltaT=1e-6, writeInterval=0.05).' advice="Use the counterFlowFlame2D tutorial case as a direct template and modify the domain dimensions, inlet velocities, species list, and time-control parameters to match the user's specifications."</similar_case_advice>
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    (0    0    0)        // 0
    (0.02 0    0)        // 1
    (0.02 0.02 0)        // 2
    (0    0.02 0)        // 3
    (0    0    0.001)    // 4
    (0.02 0    0.001)    // 5
    (0.02 0.02 0.001)    // 6
    (0    0.02 0.001)    // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (40 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    fuel
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    air
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-06;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-04;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

combustionModel  laminar;

active          true;

laminarCoeffs
{
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='reactions', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      reactions;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

species
(
    CH4
    O2
    N2
    H2O
    CO2
);

reactions
{
    methaneReaction
    {
        type        irreversibleArrheniusReaction;
        reaction    "CH4 + 2O2 = CO2 + 2H2O";
        A           5.012e11;
        beta        0;
        Ta          24358;
    }
}

{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      chemistryProperties;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

chemistryType
{
    solver          ode;
    method          chemistryModel;
}

chemistry       on;

initialChemicalTimeStep 1e-07;

odeCoeffs
{
    solver          seulex;
    absTol          1e-12;
    relTol          1e-01;
}

reactions
{
    species
    (
        CH4
        {
            specie
            {
                molWeight       16.0428;
            }
            thermodynamics
            {
                Tlow            200;

<Review_Analysis>
## Error Diagnosis

The error message is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types:
4
(
pureMixture
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture
multiComponentMixture
valueMultiComponentMixture
)
```

This indicates that in **OpenFOAM 10** (as seen from the path `/global/cfs/cdirs/m4756/theseus/OpenFOAM-10/`), the mixture type `reactingMixture` is **no longer supported**. The Foundation version of OpenFOAM (v10) restructured how reacting mixtures work — the `reactingMixture` type has been replaced.

Additionally, looking at the current configuration, there are several other inconsistencies between the setup and the user requirements:

### Issues Identified:

1. **`physicalProperties`**: The `mixture` sub-dictionary uses `reactingMixture`, which is invalid in OF10.
2. **`combustionProperties`**: While this may still work, the overall thermophysical properties need updating.
3. **`blockMeshDict`**: The third face of the outlet patch lists `(0 4 7 3)` which represents the left face (z=0 plane), but since this is a 2D case with `frontAndBack` set to `empty`, the z-direction faces should only have two faces per block side. This face doesn't exist in the mesh geometry. It should be removed or corrected.
4. **`0/U`**: The outlet boundary condition uses `pressureInletOutletVelocity`, which is fine, but verify compatibility with OF10.
5. **Other field files (`Y*, T, p`)**: These generally look correct for OF10 syntax.

## Recommended Fixes

### 1. Fix `constant/physicalProperties` — Change mixture type

Replace `reactingMixture` with `multiComponentMixture`:

```diff
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    transport       sutherland;
    thermo          janaf;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
}
```

Also remove the following lines since they reference the old chemistry reader approach that isn't needed once the chemistry is embedded in the thermo file:

Remove:
```
inertSpecie N2;
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";
foamChemistryThermoFile "<constant>/thermo.compressibleGas";
```

Instead, add the species list directly into `physicalProperties`:

```diff
species
(
    CH4
    O2
    N2
    H2O
    CO2
);
```

And include the full thermodynamic data blocks for each species (copy them from `thermo.compressibleGas`). Essentially, merge the contents of `thermo.compressibleGas` into `physicalProperties`.

### 2. Fix `constant/combustionProperties`

Add the required entries for OpenFOAM 10:

```diff
combustionModel  laminar;

active          true;

laminarCoeffs
{
    sigma           0.5;
}

gasConstant       8.3143;
```

Note: In OF10, the `laminarCoeffs` entry expects `sigma`. Also ensure `gasConstant` is defined.

### 3. Fix `system/blockMeshDict` — Remove non-existent face

The outlet patch contains three faces: `(1 2 6 5)`, `(0 4 7 3)`. The second face `(0 4 7 3)` corresponds to the left wall (x=0), which is neither an inlet nor an outlet. Since the fuel inlet is on the bottom `(0 1 5 4)` and air inlet is on the top `(3 7 6 2)`, the outlet should only be the right wall `(1 2 6 5)`. Remove the `(0 4 7 3)` face from the outlet patch.

### Summary of Changes

| File | Issue | Fix |
|------|-------|-----|
| `constant/physicalProperties` | Invalid `reactingMixture` type | Change to `multiComponentMixture` and embed species thermo data directly |
| `system/blockMeshDict` | Non-existent outlet face | Remove `(0 4 7 3)` from outlet patch |
| `constant/combustionProperties` | Missing required entries | Add `laminarCoeffs { sigma 0.5; }` and `gasConstant 8.3143;` |

These changes align with the OpenFOAM 10 architecture while preserving all user-specified parameters (domain size, species, velocities, times, etc.).
</Review_Analysis>

<Review_Analysis>
## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword defaultSpecie is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/physicalProperties"
```

This means OpenFOAM 10 expects a `defaultSpecie` keyword in the `physicalProperties` dictionary. Looking at the reference case `counterFlowFlame2D` and the OpenFOAM 10 documentation, when using `multiComponentMixture`, the `physicalProperties` dictionary needs to specify which species is the default (the one used for calculations like speed of sound).

## Analysis

Looking at the current `physicalProperties` file, it contains the species list and their thermodynamic data, but it's missing the `defaultSpecie` keyword. Additionally, the file structure has `multiComponentMixture` correctly set, but the overall file header says `object thermoPhysicalProperties` instead of `object physicalProperties` — this mismatch may also cause issues.

## Proposed Fixes

### 1. Fix `constant/physicalProperties` — Add `defaultSpecie`

Add the `defaultSpecie` keyword at the top level of the `physicalProperties` dictionary, pointing to one of the listed species. For counterflow flames, `CH4` is typically the default since it's the fuel being consumed.

The fix should be:

```
physicalProperties
{
    ... existing content ...

    defaultSpecie CH4;
}
```

So the updated file header would be:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    transport       sutherland;
    thermo          janaf;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
}

species
(
    CH4
    O2
    N2
    H2O
    CO2
);

defaultSpecie CH4;

CH4
{
    ...
}
// ... rest of species data blocks ...
```

### 2. Rename the object in the FoamFile header

Change `object thermoPhysicalProperties` to `object physicalProperties` in the `thermo.compressibleGas` file inside `constant/`. Actually, looking more carefully — the file is named `thermo.compressibleGas` but it's being referenced as `physicalProperties` through the `foamChemistryThermoFile` entry in the old approach. Since we've now embedded everything directly into `physicalProperties`, the `thermo.compressibleGas` file is no longer needed and can be safely removed. But if keeping it, make sure the object name matches.

However, the primary fix is adding `defaultSpecie CH4;` to `physicalProperties`.

## Summary

| File | Issue | Fix |
|------|-------|-----|
| `constant/physicalProperties` | Missing `defaultSpecie` keyword | Add `defaultSpecie CH4;` after the `species` list |

This is consistent with the counterFlowFlame2D tutorial case, where `defaultSpecie` is specified to indicate the reference species for thermodynamic calculations. The default species is typically the fuel (CH4) for counterflow flame simulations.
</Review_Analysis>

## Error Diagnosis

```

)
```

This occurs in `constant/chemistryProperties`. In **OpenFOAM 10** (Foundation version), the `chemistryType` dictionary has changed. The `method` keyword value `standard` is no longer recognized. The only supported method type is `chemistryModel`.

## Analysis

Looking at the current `chemistryProperties`:

```
chemistryType
{
    solver          ode;
    method          standard;
}
```

In OpenFOAM 10, the `method` keyword must be set to `chemistryModel` (not `standard`), and the `solver` keyword within `chemistryType` may also need adjustment. The supported format in OF10 is different from earlier versions.

## Proposed Fix

### 1. Fix `constant/chemistryProperties` — Update `chemistryType`

Change the `method` from `standard` to `chemistryModel`:

```
chemistryType
{
    solver          ode;
    method          chemistryModel;
}
```

That's the only change needed. The rest of the file (`chemistry on;`, `odeCoeffs` section, etc.) should remain as-is since those are unaffected by this particular error.

### Summary

| File | Issue | Fix |
|------|-------|-----|
| `constant/chemistryProperties` | `method standard` is not supported in OF10 | Change `method` to `method chemistryModel` |

This is consistent with the OpenFOAM 10 Foundation version restructuring — the chemistry method keyword was renamed/reorganized. The `chemistryModel` method type is the only supported option, and it works with the existing `odeCoeffs` structure already present in the file.

<Review_Analysis>
## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword reactions is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/chemistryProperties"
```

This means OpenFOAM 10 expects a `reactions` sub-dictionary to be defined **inside** `chemistryProperties`, not just having `chemistryType`, `chemistry on;`, and `odeCoeffs`.

In OpenFOAM 10 (Foundation version), the `chemistryProperties` dictionary structure has changed. It now requires a `reactions` entry that points to the reactions dictionary, rather than relying solely on the `foamChemistryReader`/`foamChemistryFile` approach that was used in older versions.

## Analysis

Looking at the current `chemistryProperties`:

```
chemistryType
{
    solver          ode;
    method          chemistryModel;
}

chemistry       on;

initialChemicalTimeStep 1e-07;

odeCoeffs
{
    solver          seulex;
    absTol          1e-12;
    relTol          1e-01;
}
```

In OpenFOAM 10, the solver looks for a `reactions` sub-dictionary within `chemistryProperties` to know where to find the reaction definitions. The old-style `foamChemistryReader`/`foamChemistryFile` references are handled differently now — the reactions need to be directly embedded or included via the new format.

## Proposed Fix

### Add a `reactions` sub-dictionary to `constant/chemistryProperties`

Inside the `chemistryProperties` file, add a `reactions` entry that includes the species list and the reactions dictionary. There are two approaches:

#### Approach (Recommended): Embed the reactions directly

Add the following to `constant/chemistryProperties`:

```
reactions
{
    species
    (
        CH4
        O2
        N2
        H2O
        CO2
    );

    reactions
    {
        methaneReaction
        {
            type        irreversibleArrheniusReaction;
            reaction    "CH4 + 2O2 = CO2 + 2H2O";
            A           5.012e11;
            beta        0;
            Ta          24358;
        }
    }
}
```

This combines the species list and reaction definition directly into `chemistryProperties`, eliminating the need for the separate `reactions` file and the `foamChemistryReader`/`foamChemistryFile`/`foamChemistryThermoFile` entries in `physicalProperties`.

#### Alternative: Reference the external reactions file

If you prefer to keep the `reactions` file separate, you could add:

```
reactions
{
    include
    {
        constant/reactions;
    }
}
```

But in OpenFOAM 10, the `include` directive may also require additional formatting. The safest approach is to **embed** the reactions directly into `chemistryProperties` as shown above.

### Summary

| File | Issue | Fix |
|------|-------|-----|
| `constant/chemistryProperties` | Missing `reactions` sub-dictionary | Add a `reactions` block containing the species list and the methaneReaction definition |

This is consistent with how OpenFOAM 10 handles chemistry models — the reactions must be accessible directly within the `chemistryProperties` dictionary context. The previous standalone `reactions` file is no longer automatically discovered by the solver in this version.
</Review_Analysis>
</Attempt>

## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
Attempt to return primitive entry ITstream : .../constant/chemistryProperties/reactions/species, lines 30-36
... as a sub-dictionary
```

This means OpenFOAM 10 expects the `species` entry under `reactions` to be a **sub-dictionary**, not a list of words. In OpenFOAM 10 (Foundation version), the `reactions` dictionary structure has changed — the `species` keyword now expects a dictionary mapping species names to their thermodynamic data, rather than a simple list.

## Analysis

Looking at the current `chemistryProperties`:

```
reactions
{
    species
    (
        CH4
        O2
        N2
        H2O
        CO2
    );

    reactions
    {
        methaneReaction
        {
            type        irreversibleArrheniusReaction;
            reaction    "CH4 + 2O2 = CO2 + 2H2O";
            A           5.012e11;
            beta        0;
            Ta          24358;
        }
    }
}
```

In OpenFOAM 10, the `species` entry under `reactions` should contain the actual species thermodynamic data dictionaries (like what's currently in `physicalProperties`), not a simple list. The solver tries to call `.dict()` on the `species` entry expecting it to be a dictionary, but it's a list.

## Proposed Fix

### Modify `constant/chemistryProperties` — Change the `species` entry format

Under the `reactions` sub-dictionary, replace the list-based `species` with a dictionary format that maps each species name to its thermodynamic data. Based on the structure from `physicalProperties`, the `species` entry should look like:

This moves the entire species thermodynamic data blocks (currently in `physicalProperties`) directly into `chemistryProperties` under the `reactions` dictionary, structured so that `species` becomes a proper sub-dictionary (each species name maps to its data block), satisfying the `.dict()` lookup expectation.

### Summary

| File | Issue | Fix |
|------|-------|-----|
| `constant/chemistryProperties` | `species` under `reactions` is a list, but OF10 expects a dictionary | Replace the list format with individual species sub-dictionaries containing their full thermodynamic data blocks |

This is consistent with OpenFOAM 10's restructured chemistry model format, where the `reactions` dictionary contains properly nested sub-dictionaries for species data rather than flat lists. Each species gets its own sub-dictionary with `specie`, `thermodynamics`, and `transport` entries.
